title: Eval corpus case (one file per case; lifecycle-staged requiredness)
$comment: SHAPE ONLY (engine). Instances: .sdlc-core/corpus/**/*.case.json. Dialect: the pinned sdlc_schemas keyword subset -- if/then conditions over 1-2 properties with const/enum values. Staging: capture requires only {schema,id,status,lineage,input}; annotated/active/retired additionally require {kind,determinism} (classified before AUTHORING = before annotation); active additionally requires the kind payload. Only active cases execute.
x-consumers: EE.1 loader (ee run): validates every case at load; ANY invalid case aborts the run loudly; failures[].id in latest.json is case.id (eval.yml.tmpl:75-88 reads it verbatim)
scope filter: --scope harness-only runs determinism==deterministic only; skipped judged/stochastic cases are NAMED in the report (Loop-4 exit)
EE.7: judge.rubric -> resolve_rubric() admissibility; inadmissible => case skips loudly
EE.12 stats: sampling.n/pass_at for stochastic cases (default n from standards checks[eval.stochastic_runs]); deterministic + anti_case flips always fail (derived from determinism+kind)
EE.13 lifecycle: status drives the run set; corpus health = anti-case ratio, lineage mix, status counts
EE.5 trace runner: trace.checks map 1:1 to the six shipped trace check implementations
release gate: an anti_case whose payload evaluation PASSES fails the corpus release
link pass: id corpus-unique (E-LINK-DUPLICATE); expected.tolerance_metric -> metric namespace; judge.rubric -> rubric namespace; rubric probe_tag -> tags of active anti_case cases
x-changelog: @1 2026-07: initial shape (E1.7 proposed-v1; lifecycle staging + loadable anti_case payload)

id.x-consumer: failures[].id in latest.json + PR comment; corpus-unique via link pass

kind.x-consumer: EE runner dispatch; CLOSED taxonomy (plan 6.1) -- new kind needs an ADR + schema bump

determinism.x-consumer: scope filter; flip policy (deterministic flips always fail); sampling legality. Required from annotated: classified before authoring, structurally

status.x-consumer: EE.13 lifecycle run-set selection + health metrics; staging pivot for requiredness

route.x-consumer: corpus health lineage-mix metric; capture routes

ref.x-consumer: triage back-link (incident/PR id) for non-authored routes

tags.x-consumer: selection labels; rubric meta_eval.probe_tag resolves against tags of active anti_case cases (link pass)

input.x-consumer: kind-interpreted input envelope; each kind runner validates its own input contract at load

target_kind.x-consumer: anti_case only: the kind whose evaluation must FAIL

expected_verdict.x-consumer: release gate inversion: payload evaluation passing fails the corpus release

matcher.x-consumer: matcher registry lookup at load (seeded from eval_runner.md built-ins); unknown matcher fails loudly

args.x-consumer: matcher-specific params, validated by the matcher at registration

checks.x-consumer: assertion/scenario verdict checks; minItems 1 kills check-nothing cases

ref.x-consumer: golden artefact path relative to corpus root

tolerance_metric.x-consumer: metric-library id; numeric golden compares reuse metrics[<id>].tolerance so G4 and G5 share one number; link pass -> E-LINK-UNRESOLVED

seed.x-consumer: fixes generation for deterministic property cases

checks.x-consumer: the six shipped trace check implementations (plan 6.1, closed)

rubric.x-consumer: resolve_rubric() admissibility; link pass proves the (id,version) exists

n.x-consumer: EE.12 n-run sampling; default from standards checks[eval.stochastic_runs]

sampling.x-consumer: EE.12 stochastic-only override; forbidden on deterministic cases

input.x-consumer: payload input override; defaults to the parent case input

payload.x-consumer: anti_case body: ONLY the target kind's evaluation fields (per-target_kind required via allOf). Loader synthesizes an envelope from the parent case (id '<parent>.payload', kind=target_kind, parent determinism/status/lineage, payload.input or parent input) and re-validates; the engine passes the anti_case iff evaluating that synthesized case FAILS

x-valid-cases.name: cheap-captured-breach-blob
x-valid-cases.note: Lifecycle staging: capture requires no kind/determinism/payload -- one cheap blob from a production breach (EE.13 economics).

input.raw: report omitted the Risks section entirely

x-valid-cases.name: anti-case-vacuous-output
x-valid-cases.note: Loadable under the payload rule: payload carries exactly the target kind's evaluation fields.

x-anti-cases.name: active-golden-missing-expected
x-anti-cases.note: The inventory's red-test twin: an active golden case with no expected output must fail validation.

x-anti-cases.name: new-kind-without-adr
x-anti-cases.note: Taxonomy is closed; a new kind needs an ADR + schema bump, not a corpus edit.

x-anti-cases.name: judged-claiming-deterministic
x-anti-cases.note: An LLM judge is never deterministic; classification discipline made structural.

x-anti-cases.name: active-case-unclassified
x-anti-cases.note: Staging graft: from annotated onward, determinism is required -- classified before authoring.

x-anti-cases.name: assertion-with-empty-checks
x-anti-cases.note: A case that checks nothing always passes -- vacuous green.

x-anti-cases.name: deterministic-case-with-sampling
x-anti-cases.note: Resampling a deterministic case burns budget and masks misclassification.

x-anti-cases.name: anti-case-payload-missing-target-field
x-anti-cases.note: Payload must carry the target kind's evaluation fields or the release-gate inversion has nothing to run.
